Name: efficient_inference_optimization
Title: Practical and Measurable Optimization of Language Model Inference on Commodity GPUs
Short Hypothesis: By focusing on hardware-faithful optimization techniques such as quantization, sparsity, and cache-friendly memory management, it is possible to achieve significant improvements in the inference speed of large language models on commodity GPUs without compromising accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works like HGCA, PLAIN, and MI-LLM have explored various aspects of optimizing language model inference, including hybrid CPU-GPU attention mechanisms, mixed-precision quantization, and Processing-In-Memory architectures. However, these approaches often require specialized hardware or significant modifications to the model architecture. In contrast, our proposal focuses on practical, empirically-driven optimizations that can be applied to existing models and commodity GPUs, emphasizing the importance of measurable, reproducible performance gains.
Abstract: This research proposal aims to investigate practical and measurable optimization techniques for improving the inference speed of large language models on commodity GPUs. By applying empirical methods such as quantization, sparsity, and cache-friendly memory management, we seek to achieve significant reductions in latency while maintaining model accuracy. Our approach will emphasize hardware-faithful evaluation, including detailed profiling and benchmarking, to ensure that our optimizations are grounded in real-world performance improvements. The goal is to provide a set of actionable, shippable optimizations that can be widely adopted in the field, contributing to the advancement of efficient language model inference on readily available hardware.
Experiments: Quantization experiments: Evaluate the impact of different quantization schemes (e.g., int4, int8) on model accuracy and inference speed.
Sparsity experiments: Investigate the effects of sparse attention and sparse linear layers on model performance and computational efficiency.
Cache-friendly memory management experiments: Assess the benefits of optimizing memory access patterns for improved cache utilization and reduced memory bandwidth usage.
Benchmarking: Conduct thorough benchmarking of optimized models on commodity GPUs, reporting metrics such as latency, throughput, and energy efficiency.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential accuracy degradation due to quantization or sparsity.
Increased complexity in model and optimization pipeline management.
Dependence on specific hardware architectures, which might limit the generalizability of the findings.
